title: Clank & Slop — The Tape
tagline: — READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE —

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: Hormuz is open only on someone’s spreadsheet: CENTCOM counted 55 merchant ships and 17M+ barrels on June 20, but AXS Marine put verified commercial crossings at 25 on 18 June vs roughly 120 a day pre-war, and PetroChina could not fix a VLCC even near WS 650–750. A barrel can clear on price before a ship clears on risk.

The mission trade became a float trade. With a public float of about 638.9M shares (~4.9%), SPCX fell more than 6% Thursday while still 30%+ above the IPO, options ran to 1.8M contracts and the Cursor merge is priced off a seven-day VWAP. A possible bond of at least $20B is the second gear.

The budget war inside the grain war: Odesa-region export volumes are at risk of falling from about 6mn to 4mn tons a month, only about 1mn tons can be redirected to the Danube, and terminal losses since the invasion total $1.5bn against a repair queue that competes with winter-grid spending.

The Qwable leak sits outside the perimeter: the alleged Fable 5 distill is unproven, but it exposes the core flaw in frontier-model export control — access can be revoked, traces cannot. AWS revocation, JPMorgan and Goldman restrictions and G7 "trusted partners" talk all operate on future access, not past outputs.

props.title: Three Hormuz counts, three realities
props.subtitle: Strait transit reality · vessels per day
props.source: CENTCOM (55 merchant ships, June 20), Reuters / AXS Marine (25 commercial crossings 18 Jun; ~120 pre-war), UKMTO / JMIC (central route mined)

items.why: Pakistan says Sunday talks begin with Qatari mediators; whether instructions get written on custody, inspection and timing tests the compliance-room read against the missing-party read.
A military transit count is not a commercial one; the desk watches eastbound and westbound flows, VLCC fixtures, war-risk premiums and the JMIC advisory, not the communiqué.
Up to 20% of the 180-day shares (~940M, ~1.5× the float) can release after Q2; a further 10% if SPCX closes 30% above IPO — the squeeze created the price, the price may release the supply.
Consideration in SPCX stock off a seven-day VWAP at a $60.0B implied value; a higher tape means less dilution, so the ticker helps set the deal’s mechanics.

items.what: Odesa terminals vs grid repair queue
items.why: With $1.5bn of terminal losses and budget resources focused on the power sector before winter, the strikes transmit into FX receipts and carryover stocks before any clean global grain-price chart.

items.what: War-risk insurance verified at as high as 3% of hull value earlier in the conflict, with Lloyd’s List putting some Gulf premiums at roughly 10 times pre-strike levels — a lower Brent tape does not automatically strip out the abnormal insurance structure.

Commerce Secretary Lutnick’s letter cited diversion risk to military-intelligence users in China and Russia; the verifiable order is on access, while the distillation premise comes from Anthropic’s own capability argument, not a public BIS text — the gap the alleged Qwable artifact sits in.

More than 500,000 SpaceX options traded in the first hour and over a million by early afternoon; in a small-float stock, heavy derivatives turn price into a lever on the Cursor deal, not just a readout.
